source: CAS Academic Policies §Pass/Fail Option (heading line 408; career-cap line 410); §Major / Minor Restrictions (line 138)
body:
**No, Pass/Fail is not allowed for major or minor courses in CAS.**

The CAS bulletin (Academic Policies, line 138) is explicit: *"No course to be counted toward the major or minor may be taken on a Pass/Fail basis."* This applies to every CAS major.

If you take a course P/F and later try to apply it to your major or minor, you will need to retake it for a letter grade. Students who change majors may also lose credit for previously P/F-graded courses if those courses become required for the new major.

A few related notes:
- P/F also doesn't count toward CAS Core Curriculum requirements (line 94), with one exception: foreign-language sequence courses BELOW the Intermediate II level may be taken P/F.
- The career P/F cap is 32 credits, and you may elect at most one P/F per term (line 410).
